things.name: Vasa Museum
things.description: The extraordinary 17th-century warship Vasa, salvaged after 333 years at the bottom of Stockholm harbour

things.name: Gamla Stan (Old Town)
things.description: One of Europe's best-preserved medieval city centres on an island — colourful houses and Nobel Museum

things.name: ABBA Museum
things.description: Interactive museum dedicated to Sweden's most iconic pop group with holograms, costumes and recording booths

things.name: Royal Palace (Kungliga Slottet)
things.description: One of the world's largest royal palaces — the official residence of the Swedish royal family

things.name: Skansen Open Air Museum
things.description: The world's oldest open air museum (1891) with 150 historic buildings and Nordic wildlife

things.name: Fotografiska Photography Museum
things.description: World-class photography museum in a converted Art Nouveau customs house with a rooftop restaurant

things.name: Djurgarden Island
things.description: Royal national city park island housing the Vasa, Skansen, ABBA Museum and Nordic Museum

things.name: Stockholm City Hall (Stadshuset)
things.description: The 1923 landmark where the Nobel Prize banquet is held — climb the tower for panoramic views

things.name: Sodermalm District
things.description: Stockholm's coolest neighbourhood on a rocky island — vintage shops, indie cafes and Stieg Larsson country

things.name: Stockholm Archipelago Boat Trip
things.description: 30,000 islands — day cruises through the archipelago from Stromkajen pier are a quintessential Stockholm experience
